body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Closes https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/issues/760


## To-do
[X] verify runs in CI


## Content
1. Uses built in composition capability of ClimaUtilities, instead of the pre-composed vars in the _clima.nc files
2. Writes a helper function to make the prescribed atmos, radiation, and lai objects for ERA5 data. I kept LAI separate since we may want to use MODIS data for that instead.

Note that: We cant test the helper function with github unit tests because we dont have era5 data accessible there (only on caltech cluster). But we do use the function on buildkite CI, so it seems OK/it is tested

- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

title: composition of era5 data and helper functions

body:
## Purpose 
Currently, 6x per step - in the tendency function of soil and soil CO2 -  we allocate a 2d surface field of type WVector{FT}. This PR adds space to the cache so we don't do this allocation. Not a big deal, but an easy fix.

Canopy and Snow do not use ClimaCore operators so they are not affected by this PR.

We also added the same var to the bucket model cache, but note that the bucket does not the same `boundary_vars...` functionality to add variables to the cache. so we add it directly to `auxiliary_vars()`
## To-do
Benchmark after merging/rebasing off of #823 


## Content
Adds :top_bc_wvec and :bottom_bc_wvec to the cache. Note that for soil Energy Hydrology, we use the same cache spot for both heat and water BC (no need to store both at the same time).

title: clean up benchmarking runs

body:
## Purpose 
Address inconsistencies in naming for radiation: upwelling/downwelling vs in/out

Store the albedo, emissivity, and effective temperature of the land

Partially addresses OKR2.3.8

## To-do



## Content
We now follow the convention everywhere:
- downwelling "_d" fluxes are positive if down
- upwelling "_u" fluxes are positive if up
- store alpha_sfc, epsilon_sfc, and T_sfc in the cache. These are computed using SW_u, SW_d, LW_u, and the Stefan-Boltzmann constant.


<!---
